highlights: Board members will meet in closed session to discuss candidates for principal positions at one or more CISD campuses, with any hiring decisions to be made in open session.
The Board will receive a legal update from district attorneys regarding pending or contemplated litigation in closed session.

The Board will take public action following closed session to give the Superintendent direction on a student safety matter, though no details about the specific issue have been disclosed in the agenda.

The Board will formally recognize a College Park High School athlete who won the UIL Class 6A Boys 1600-meter state title and a Woodlands High School athlete who won the UIL Class 6A Girls Tennis Singles state championship.
The Board will consider approving an application for TEA's 2026–2027 Reading/Language Arts (RLA) Blended Pilot Grant, which would fund a blended learning approach to literacy instruction.
Campus principals will be recognized by the Board, likely in connection with the end of the school year or leadership transitions.
Board members and the Superintendent will participate in Team of Eight governance training as part of ongoing leadership development.
The board authorized the superintendent to settle a dispute with SI Environmental LLC for up to $161,000, approved 6-1.
The Board Operating Procedures Manual was adopted 4-3, over objections from Trustee Horton and two others regarding provisions expanding board president authority.
The board approved updates to Board Policies DBD, FEA, FEC, and GC.
The board voted 7-0 to make no change to the placement of *The Action Bible: Heroes and Villains*, keeping the book in district libraries.
The board approved the 2025 library materials acquisition list 7-0, following a 30-day public review period.

Five Bond 2023 construction bid awards were approved 7-0, covering HVAC, roofing, and safety upgrades at multiple campuses, plus gym additions at Anderson and Glen Loch Elementaries ($22,000,000) and David and Galatas Elementaries ($13,389,623).
The board approved a $28,646,253 GMP for major renovations at San Jacinto Elementary and additions at Ross Elementary, 7-0.
A $25,000,000 bid for HVAC replacements at Caney Creek High School and security upgrades at 13 other campuses was awarded to Ellesore Constructors, approved 6-1.
